summary: The q5 dataset context provides only aggregate issue counts, showing a uniform distribution of exactly 100 issues across all ten problems (q1-q10) with no per-problem differentiation or detailed proof status. Without granular data on issue types, recency, or progress, no problem currently stands out in activity, traction, or solvability. Additional metadata is required to generate actionable, problem-specific intelligence.
problems: All problems q1-q10 report an identical count of exactly 100 issues, suggesting a possible cap, placeholder, or data-export artifact rather than genuine differentiated activity.
No per-problem breakdown of issue categories (e.g., logical gaps, notation, missing lemmas) is available, preventing pattern analysis.
Proof status labels are missing despite a 'Proof Status' header, so solvability cannot be assessed for any problem.
No timestamps or activity recency data are present, making it impossible to identify which problems have the most traction.
The 'Issues Per Problem' section is empty, indicating incomplete or malformed dataset ingestion.
highlights: Complete coverage: all ten problems in the q5 dataset have associated issue records, indicating no problem has been overlooked.
Consistent data structure across problems will simplify automated parsing once richer fields are added.
The uniform 100-issue count provides a clear baseline for measuring future reduction as proofs mature.

suggested_todos.title: Investigate and resolve the uniform 100-issue count, which likely indicates a data cap or export bug
Enrich the dataset with per-problem issue categories, severity, and timestamps to enable traction analysis
Populate the missing proof status labels so solvability of each problem can be evaluated
Once corrected data is available, re-run the insight report to identify top-priority problems for the week
Establish a baseline dashboard tracking issue counts per problem over time
